goal: Remove all shell scripts and ensure the Go version of aether works perfectly, we are finalising the system, also ensure to remove all parts of the GSD system from this repo, this has to be done with extreme care and safety

phases.name: Commit Audit
phases.description: Review commits 3019dec to d1754ea for correctness — verify Stage Audit Gate, exchange shouldExclude tests, state-api DATA_DIR fix, packaging integrity, and cross-reference consistency

tasks.goal: Audit Stage Audit Gate implementation in build-complete.md and build.yaml — verify the gate halts when prerequisite stages are missing and cannot be bypassed
Audit exchange shouldExclude tests in update-transaction.test.js — verify the 3 tests prove .sh permitted, .xml/.json blocked, and directory not blocked
Audit state-api.sh DATA_DIR fix — trace $DATA_DIR vs $COLONY_DATA_DIR usage and verify consistency across all COLONY_STATE.json references
Run full verification suite (npm test, npm run lint:sync, npm run lint) and verify cross-reference consistency between playbooks, YAML sources, generated commands, and agent definitions

success_criteria: All commits in range audited with individual verdicts
npm test, npm run lint:sync, npm run lint all pass cleanly
Findings documented with any issues flagged

phases.name: Trust Scoring Engine and Event Bus Foundation
phases.description: Build the two foundational infrastructure components: trust scoring engine (40/35/25 weighted formula, 60-day half-life, 0.2 floor, 7 tiers) and JSONL event bus (pub/sub, TTL cleanup)

tasks.goal: Create trust scoring engine at .aether/utils/trust-scoring.sh — trust-calculate, trust-decay, trust-tier subcommands with 40/35/25 source/evidence/activity weights and 60-day half-life
Create JSONL event bus at .aether/utils/event-bus.sh — event-publish, event-subscribe, event-cleanup, event-replay subcommands with TTL-based cleanup and file-lock.sh for concurrency
Register trust-scoring.sh and event-bus.sh in aether-utils.sh dispatcher — add source lines, case entries, and templates

success_criteria: trust-scoring.sh and event-bus.sh exist with working subcommands
Both registered in dispatcher and callable via aether-utils.sh
tests/bash/test-trust-scoring.sh and tests/bash/test-event-bus.sh pass

phases.name: Trust-Scored Storage and Graph Layer
phases.description: Upgrade observation storage with trust scores, create standalone instincts.json with full schema, and build jq-based graph layer for instinct relationships

tasks.goal: Upgrade learning-observe in learning.sh to compute and store trust_score, source_type, and compression_level — backward-compatible migration for existing 62 entries

Create standalone instincts.json with full schema (trust_score, trust_tier, provenance, application_history, related_instincts) — expose instinct-store, instinct-read-trusted, instinct-decay-all, instinct-archive subcommands
Create jq-based graph layer at .aether/utils/graph.sh — graph-link, graph-neighbors, graph-reach, graph-cluster subcommands for instinct relationship traversal

success_criteria: Observations carry trust scores on write
instincts.json exists as standalone file with full schema
Graph traversal works for 1-2 hop queries via jq
All test files pass

phases.name: Curation Ants
phases.description: Implement 8 curation ant scripts in .aether/utils/curation-ants/ — nurse, herald, librarian, archivist, sentinel, janitor, scribe, critic — with curation-run orchestrator

tasks.goal: Create 4 core curation ants: nurse.sh (trust recalculation), herald.sh (QUEEN.md promotion at trust >= 0.75), librarian.sh (inventory stats), critic.sh (contradiction detection via graph)
Create 4 operational curation ants: sentinel.sh (health monitoring), janitor.sh (expired event/archive pruning), archivist.sh (low-trust archival), scribe.sh (markdown report generation)
Register all 8 ants in dispatcher and create curation-run orchestrator with correct execution order: Sentinel → Nurse → Critic → Herald → Janitor → Archivist → Librarian → Scribe

success_criteria: 8 scripts exist in .aether/utils/curation-ants/
All registered in dispatcher
curation-run orchestrator executes end-to-end
Test files pass

phases.name: Consolidation Pipeline Integration
phases.description: Wire the stack into Aether lifecycle — lightweight consolidation at /ant-continue, full consolidation at /ant-seal, plus end-to-end integration test

tasks.goal: Create consolidation-phase-end subcommand (runs Nurse + Herald + Janitor) and integrate into continue-finalize.md after learning extraction
Create consolidation-seal subcommand (runs full curation-run + final trust-decay-all + archival) and integrate into seal flow before hive-promote
Create end-to-end integration test covering full pipeline: observe → trust score → promote to instinct → graph link → curation-run → herald promotion to QUEEN.md → consolidation

success_criteria: Phase-end consolidation integrated into continue flow
Seal consolidation integrated into seal flow
E2E test passes covering full pipeline
No regressions in existing test suite

phases.name: Documentation and Full Test Sweep
phases.description: Document the Structural Learning Stack, update CLAUDE.md, and verify complete test suite passes with zero regressions

tasks.goal: Create architecture documentation at .aether/docs/structural-learning-stack.md covering pipeline stages, trust formula, 7 tiers, curation ant responsibilities, and integration points
Update CLAUDE.md with new component counts, architecture references, and Wisdom Pipeline stages
Run complete test suite (all existing + 9 new test files) and verify zero regressions

success_criteria: Architecture doc exists at .aether/docs/structural-learning-stack.md
CLAUDE.md updated with accurate counts
Full test suite passes with zero regressions

phase_learnings.phase_name: Enforce non-skippable playbook execution in build orchestrators

learnings.claim: Adding pre-synthesis verification gates to build orchestrators catches incomplete builds before synthesis — gate should name all prerequisite stages explicitly
learnings.evidence: All 6 success criteria passed on first build attempt, watcher quality 9/10

learnings.claim: Running lint:sync and lint as a dedicated verification phase after code changes confirms no sync drift — useful as a final gate before colony seal
learnings.evidence: lint:sync confirmed 45+45 commands and 24 agents in sync, lint (shellcheck+json+sync) all clean, 524 tests pass

learnings.claim: Parallel swarm audits (4 scouts) are effective for commit review — each scout can deeply examine a specific area while cross-referencing findings
learnings.evidence: 4 scouts produced structured verdicts for Stage Audit Gate, Exchange Tests, State API, and Cross-Reference consistency

learnings.claim: Untracked test files can encode wrong assumptions — test-colony-data-dir.sh expected COLONY_DATA_DIR for COLONY_STATE.json but the architecture requires DATA_DIR
learnings.evidence: Scout 3 traced _resolve_colony_data_dir bootstrap chain and confirmed COLONY_STATE.json must stay at DATA_DIR root

learnings.claim: Standalone instinct storage with full schema (trust_score, trust_tier, provenance, application_history, related_instincts) is cleaner than embedding instincts in COLONY_STATE.json — enables independent queries and decay without touching colony state
learnings.evidence: instinct-store.sh with 16 tests passing, instinct-read-trusted/decay-all/archive all functional, 50-instinct cap with automatic archival

learnings.claim: jq-based graph layer provides adequate relationship traversal for instinct networks up to a few hundred edges — BFS reach with max-hops=3 cap prevents pathological queries
learnings.evidence: graph.sh with graph-link/neighbors/reach/cluster all functional, union-find clustering works for current scale

learnings.claim: Backward-compatible migration for existing observations (legacy trust_score backfill at 0.49) lets new fields coexist with old data without breaking reads
learnings.evidence: learning-observe now computes trust_score on write, legacy migration fills 62 existing entries with 0.49 default, all 524 tests pass

instincts.action: Stage Audit Gate pattern: add pre-synthesis verification gate to build orchestrators ensures all stages complete before synthesis (evidence: all 6 success criteria passed, watcher quality 9/10)
Parallel swarm audits with 4 scouts are effective for commit review
Untracked test files can encode wrong assumptions about architecture
Existing observations now recalculate trust_score on re-observation via trust-calculate with source_type/evidence_type/days_since (evidence: 34 tests pass, watcher quality 9/10)

Modifying existing code (learning.sh) and verifying new modules (graph.sh, instinct-store.sh) can safely run in parallel when modifications are additive (evidence: 3 parallel builders, 0 merge conflicts)
Standalone instinct storage with full schema is cleaner than embedding instincts in COLONY_STATE.json
jq-based graph layer provides adequate relationship traversal for instinct networks
Backward-compatible migration for existing observations lets new fields coexist with old data

instincts.trigger: commit audit phase with multiple areas to review
instincts.action: deploy parallel swarm scouts (4+), each covering a specific audit area
evidence: Swarm swarm-1774988242 completed 4 parallel audits in less time than sequential review

instincts.action: use standalone storage files with full schema (trust_score, provenance, application_history) rather than embedding in colony state — enables independent queries, decay, and archival without touching state
evidence: instinct-store.sh with 16 tests, 50-instinct cap with auto-archival, all 524 tests pass

instincts.trigger: adding new fields to existing data structures
instincts.action: implement backward-compatible migration that backfills missing fields with safe defaults — new code reads old data, migration runs on write, no separate migration script needed
evidence: learning-observe migration filled 62 existing entries with 0.49 default trust_score without breaking any reads

instincts.trigger: untracked test files or documentation exist from a prior session
instincts.action: verify assumptions match actual architecture before trusting their assertions
evidence: test-colony-data-dir.sh encoded wrong assumption about COLONY_STATE.json location
